title: 控方证人
summary:
Sattler informs Dr. Castells that Arthur Carmichael inherited the Walden estate following the death of his father, Sir William. Arthur lives at the financially struggling estate with his stepmother, the mysterious Lady Cammichael—who is of mixed British and Asian descent—and his eight-year-old half-brother. Arthur is engaged to Miss Felicity Patterson, who is currently staying at Walden.

Upon arriving at the house, Dr. Castells observes Miss Patterson walking across the lawn with a basket of roses and a gray Persian cat. Inside, Dr. Castells meets Lady Cammichael and Miss Patterson. Miss Patterson expresses distress over Arthur’s condition, claiming he is "not human anymore," while Dr. Castells suggests the change may be due to amnesia or a secondary personality. During the conversation, Lady Cammichael reacts with hostility toward Miss Patterson and accidentally knocks over a teapot when Dr. Castells mentions the cat.

Sattler leads Dr. Castells and Miss Patterson upstairs to see Arthur, who is sitting by a window behaving strangely. Arthur observes the visitors stealthily but remains mostly motionless. When offered milk, Arthur approaches the table without using his hands, drinking directly from the cup in a manner Sattler describes as a "primitive state." This behavior leaves Miss Patterson trembling and convinced that the man is not Arthur.

Later, during dinner, Dr. Castells notes Lady Cammichael's mystical aura, though Sattler observes she is devoted to her son. After dinner, Dr. Castells hears a cat meowing outside and attempts to let it in, but the corridor is empty. The group stares at him intently following this occurrence. When Dr. Castells retires to his room, he tells Sattler the house seems normal, prompting Sattler to question if he truly finds the house charming.
